name: HR_GEN_AI_AGENTS_B
description: This table stores the Gen AI Agents

columns.Name: AGENT_ID
columns.Comments: The Unique Identifier of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: AGENT_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the Agent Code of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: FAMILY
columns.Comments: Product family of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: PRODUCT
columns.Comments: Product area of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: AGENT_CREATED_DATE
columns.Comments: Created Date of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: OWNER_ID
columns.Comments: ID of the owner who created the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: OWNER_NAME
columns.Comments: Name of the owner who created the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: STATUS
columns.Comments: Status of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: PROMPT_CODE
columns.Comments: Prompt Code of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow. FK to the prompt table

columns.Name: NAMESPACE
columns.Comments: Namespace of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: VERSION
columns.Comments: Version of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: SPECIFICATION
columns.Comments: JSON specification of the Gen AI Agent in the Agentic Workflow

columns.Name: MAX_INTERACTIONS
columns.Comments: Maximum number of interactions with LLM engine.

columns.Name: AGENT_TYPE
columns.Comments: This column stores the Agent type.

columns.Name: PROMPT
columns.Comments: Alternate prompt used by this agent.

columns.Name: MODEL_CONFIG_ID
columns.Comments: Reference to a LLM model configuration. Foreign key to FAI_MODEL_CONFIGS table.

columns.Name: INTERNAL_NAME
columns.Comments: This column represents the system name of the Agent.

columns.Name: INTERNAL_DESCRIPTION
columns.Comments: This column represents the system description of the Agent.

columns.Name: REUSABLE_FLAG
columns.Comments: This column indicates whether the Agent is resusable.

columns.Name: SEEDED_FLAG
columns.Comments: Y or N. This column differentiates seeded data from customer created.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
